examples: The diet of the affluent has not changed much over the decades.
Cigarette smoking used to be more common among affluent people.
It's a fallacy that the affluent give relatively more to charity than the less prosperous.
And the town, historically reliant on industry and crisscrossed by pockmarked streets, isn't especially affluent either.
Banks are also shifting their focus away from the mass market consumer toward the affluent.
